Okay, so a lot of people, or not a lot of people, but sometimes one of the obstacles that may come up is I just don't know if I can hit that goal. You know, 25 pounds seems like a lot, or 6% body fat seems like a lot. And so the way that we usually confront this is actually pretty simple. Like listen, the goal actually used to be lower than that. And the thing is, is like basically every single human being in the world hit it. But what we realized was that a lot of people weren't hitting it for the right reasons. And so they were stuffing their faces the night before they would weigh in and drink a bunch of water, stepping on the scale like 10 pounds heavier than they were normally. And then on their last day, cutting all their waters, not eating the last day, dehydrating themselves so that they'd be like 8 pounds lighter. You know what I mean? And so they were like plus 8 on the front end and then like minus 8 on the back end. So they'd only lost like 4 pounds, you know what I mean in the 6 weeks. So it kind of defied the purpose of the whole challenge, which is like we were trying to actually help people like transform their bodies. And so this challenge takes that into account. And so we actually, like we just now know that people are going to game it. So that's kind of built into the goal. So we know people are going to do that stuff in the beginning. We know they're going to do it at the end. And so it just takes into account so that realistically, you know, if people put on 5, 6 pounds at the beginning, lose 5 or 6 at the end. That's 12. People losing, you know, 12 pounds in 6 weeks, which is 2 pounds of actual real tissue loss, which is super standard. So hopefully that makes sense. And that's why the goals are set that way. We still have an obscenely high success rate as is. But that way the transformations are real and genuine. And actually what you guys came here for, not to like see how much water you could, how much you could stuff yourself in. The first day, which is hopefully not why you're doing it.
